activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Choose the correct descriptions of regular routines.

options.text: I walk to work most days.
Eva is taking the bus every day usually.
Our team meets on Monday mornings.
He working from home every Friday.

activities.explanation: Las rutinas se expresan con present simple: «walk» y «meets».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: You enter a kitchen while dinner is being prepared. Which sentences describe actions happening now?

options.text: Marta chops vegetables now.
Marta is chopping vegetables.
The soup is boiling on the stove.
The soup boiling on the stove.

activities.explanation: Las dos acciones están en progreso y requieren «is chopping» e «is boiling».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences correctly describe facts or stable situations?

options.text: Water boils at 100°C at sea level.
My cousins are owning a bakery.
This road connects the two villages.
The Earth is going around the Sun every year usually.

activities.explanation: Los hechos generales y conexiones estables usan present simple.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences correctly describe temporary situations this month?

options.text: I stay with friends this month.
I am staying with friends this month.
Our manager is working in Berlin for four weeks.
Our manager work in Berlin for four weeks now.

activities.explanation: La duración limitada hace natural el present continuous en ambos casos.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences correctly use verbs that normally describe states?

options.text: I am knowing the answer.
This coat belongs to Luis.
We need more chairs.
She is preferring the blue one.

activities.explanation: «Belong» y «need» describen estados y se usan aquí en present simple.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Kai normally cycles to college, but his bicycle is being repaired today.

options.text: Kai usually cycles to college.
Today he takes the bus because this is his permanent habit.
He is taking the bus today.
He taking the bus today.

activities.explanation: La rutina usa «cycles» y la excepción temporal de hoy usa «is taking».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences correctly describe official timetables?

options.text: The museum opens at ten tomorrow.
The final train leaves at 00.15.
The lecture is start at nine.
The ferry leaving every two hours.

activities.explanation: Los horarios fijados pueden expresarse con present simple aunque se refieran al futuro.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences describe changes that are happening gradually?

options.text: Food prices are rising again.
The days get shorter right now and every second.
More people are using electric bicycles.
The neighbourhood becoming quieter.

activities.explanation: Los cambios en desarrollo se expresan correctamente con «are rising» y «are using».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two questions correctly ask about the present?

options.text: Do you usually cook at home?
Are you wait for someone?
Are you waiting for someone?
Does you work nearby?

activities.explanation: La rutina requiere «Do ... cook?» y la acción actual «Are ... waiting?».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two negative sentences are formed correctly?

options.text: I don't use the car on weekdays.
He isn't understand the instructions.
They aren't staying for dinner tonight.
She doesn't working on Saturdays.

activities.explanation: Present simple usa «don't + infinitivo»; present continuous usa «aren't + -ing».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two uses of «think» are correct?

options.text: I think this route is faster.
I am thinking that this route is a fact.
We are thinking about moving closer to work.
She thinking the answer is wrong.

activities.explanation: «Think» como opinión usa simple; «think about» como proceso temporal admite continuo.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two uses of «have» are correct?

options.text: Rosa has two older brothers.
Rosa is having two older brothers.
We are having lunch at the moment.
We have lunch at the moment right now.

activities.explanation: La posesión usa «has»; la actividad «have lunch» en progreso usa «are having».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two uses of «see» are correct?

options.text: I see what you mean.
I am seeing what you mean now with my eyes.
The dentist is seeing a patient at the moment.
The dentist see a patient right now.

activities.explanation: «See» como entender es de estado; como atender o reunirse puede ser una acción en curso.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Mila has arranged two appointments for Friday. Which descriptions are correct?

options.text: She is meeting her tutor at ten.
She meets her tutor at ten every Friday, according to this one arrangement.
She is having a video call at three.
She have a video call at three.

activities.explanation: Los arreglos personales ya acordados se expresan aquí con present continuous.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences place frequency adverbs correctly?

options.text: I am usually checking email before breakfast.
I usually check email after breakfast.
Sam is always leaving dirty cups on my desk!
Sam leaves always dirty cups on my desk.

activities.explanation: «Usually» precede al verbo léxico en una rutina; «always» entre «is» y «leaving» puede expresar irritación.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: A software company is running a six-week trial. Which descriptions distinguish the trial from normal practice?

options.text: The support team normally finishes at six.
During the trial, they are working until eight.
During the trial, they work until eight as an unchanging permanent fact.
The support team is normally finish at six.

activities.explanation: La práctica habitual usa simple y la modificación limitada a seis semanas usa continuo.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences correctly contrast a property with an action?

options.text: This sauce tastes too salty.
The chef is tasting the sauce before serving it.
This sauce is tasting too salty as its permanent quality.
The chef tastes the sauce right this second usually.

activities.explanation: La propiedad usa «tastes»; la acción deliberada en progreso usa «is tasting».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences correctly contrast appearance with deliberate behaviour?

options.text: The task appears straightforward.
Luca is appearing in a play this week.
The task is appearing straightforward as a state.
Luca appears in a play right now this week temporarily, always.

activities.explanation: «Appear» como parecer describe un estado; como actuar en una obra admite continuo.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: A report compares a long-term fact with a current trend. Which sentences fit?

options.text: The region produces most of its electricity from wind.
Demand for home batteries is growing rapidly.
The region is producing most electricity as an eternal scientific fact.
Demand grows this month at the moment.

activities.explanation: El hecho relativamente estable usa simple; la tendencia actual usa continuo.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two questions distinguish a general opinion from an ongoing decision process?

options.text: What do you think of the proposal?
What are you thinking about at the moment?
What are you think of the proposal?
What do you thinking about now?

activities.explanation: La opinión usa «do ... think»; el proceso mental actual usa «are ... thinking».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences correctly use present forms for future events?

options.text: Our flight departs at 06.40 tomorrow.
I am meeting the project lead after we land.
Our flight is depart at 06.40 tomorrow.
I meet the project lead once tomorrow as a fixed transport timetable.

activities.explanation: El horario de vuelo usa simple; el arreglo personal acordado usa continuo.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences correctly distinguish measurement from an active process?

options.text: The parcel weighs just under two kilos.
The assistant is weighing the parcel now.
The parcel is weighing two kilos as its property.
The assistant weighs the parcel at this exact moment and only once.

activities.explanation: El peso como propiedad usa simple; la acción de medirlo ahora usa continuo.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: A reviewer describes a device and the test being performed now. Which sentences are correct?

options.text: The device contains two rechargeable batteries.
The engineers are testing how long they last.
The device is containing two batteries.
The engineers test them at this moment only and no other time.

activities.explanation: «Contain» describe composición; la prueba en curso requiere «are testing».

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: Which two sentences use continuous forms to signal temporary or irritating behaviour?

options.text: You're constantly interrupting me today.
Nora is being unusually quiet this morning.
Nora is being a quiet person by permanent nature.
You constantly interrupts me today.

activities.explanation: El continuo marca una conducta repetida molesta y un comportamiento temporal, no rasgos permanentes.

activities.instructions: Select the two sentences that are correct in the context.
activities.prompt: A clinic notice separates its normal service from today's disruption. Which sentences are correct?

options.text: The clinic normally accepts walk-in patients until four.
Today it is closing at two because of staff training.
The clinic is normally accepting patients as an established timetable.
Today it closes at two right now because the temporary action is in progress.

activities.explanation: La política habitual usa simple; el cambio excepcional ya previsto para hoy usa continuo.
